paper_evidence: Fig 1e: µs-ms > no-motion > ps-ns in conservation; missing-peak residues statistically INDISTINGUISHABLE from Rex residues
mechanism: µs-ms motions are overwhelmingly functional (catalysis/allostery/binding), and function is conserved

paper_evidence: Definitional: Rex = increase in R2 from µs-ms exchange
mechanism: Same physical phenomenon as the missing-peak label

feature: Proximity to functional site (active site, ligand pocket, allosteric net)
paper_evidence: Abstract + Fig 4f: catalysis & ligand binding 'particularly well predicted'; YcqA FMN loop, OMP omega-loop, APOBEC-2 specificity loop
mechanism: Function requires the very interconversion that broadens peaks

paper_evidence: Fig 5e: Adenylate Kinase hinges/lid, K-Ras Switch I/II, MptpA P-loop, DUSP3 variable-insert loop
mechanism: Lid open<->closed IS a µs-ms conformational exchange; the loop broadens even apo

feature: Conformational difference between apo vs holo structures
paper_evidence: Fig 4f MptpA: high p(exchange) in loops that differ between apo 2LUO and holo 1U2P
mechanism: Residues that move between crystallized states are the ones sampling multiple environments

paper_evidence: Fig 4c BPTI: 5 MD substates (Shaw 2010) -> Dyna-1 AUROC 0.92 for the two loops
mechanism: Where an MD ensemble visits multiple basins = where chemical environment fluctuates on µs-ms

paper_evidence: Fig 2d: missing most frequently in loops (though present across all SS types)
mechanism: Loops are conformationally labile

paper_evidence: Fig 4c BPTI Cys14/Lys15/Cys38/Arg39 known ms disulfide transitions
mechanism: A specific slow chemical-environment switch that broadens those amides

paper_evidence: Fig 5: CypA Arg148 high R2,inf; adding these labels raised AUROC in 5/6 CPMG sets (BlaC 0.65->0.72)
mechanism: Fast-µs exchange too fast for CPMG B1 fields — conventional analysis misses it, Dyna-1 flags it

paper_evidence: Fig 2h: B-factor distributions of ASSIGNED vs UNASSIGNED residues are SIMILAR
mechanism: B-factor mixes lattice/refinement/fast disorder; carries little µs-ms information

paper_evidence: Fig 2f,g: missing NMR peaks are DISTINCT from residues unresolved in X-ray/EM (little overlap)
mechanism: Unresolved density = static disorder/flexibility, a different phenomenon than µs-ms exchange

paper_evidence: Ext Data Fig 2: low pLDDT tracks ps-ns motion; little difference between no-motion and µs-ms
mechanism: pLDDT senses fast/static disorder, not slow exchange — low confidence != µs-ms dynamic

paper_evidence: Fig 3c: naive AA+DSSP classifier AUROC = 0.501 (random)
mechanism: Composition alone carries no dynamics signal without context

paper_evidence: Fig 4b / Ext Fig 7: 14/29 worst-AUROC proteins bind phosphate, measured in phosphate buffer; exchange in +charged surface residues
mechanism: Buffer ion binding creates real exchange that is NOT the protein's intrinsic function

paper_evidence: Methods: 2H entries removed; entry 25080 flagged
mechanism: Unexchanged deuterons give erroneously missing amide peaks

paper_evidence: MK12/p38gamma p(exchange) high everywhere; single field strength can't separate ps-ns from µs-ms
mechanism: Fast motion can co-occur and confuses single-field labeling

paper_evidence: NtrC Tyr101 ring-flip exchange MISSED by Dyna-1
mechanism: A µs-ms process not tied to backbone/function — a Dyna-1 blind spot

paper_evidence: Methods: 'assigned but no R1/R2/NOE' category separated out
mechanism: Overlap, not exchange — pollutes the missing label

paper_evidence: Methods: 1834 seqs had His-tags, trimmed
mechanism: Tag residues frequently unassigned for non-dynamics reasons

paper_evidence: Excluded: no backbone amide -> always 'missing'
mechanism: Not a dynamics signal at all

feature: Chemical-shift difference Δω relative to exchange rate kex
paper_evidence: p8/p12: broadening beyond detection when Δω ~ kex (intermediate exchange)
mechanism: THE governing physics: same motion is visible (fast/slow) or invisible (intermediate) depending on Δω/kex
